# Ingestion Requirements Checklist: UC-01 Scheduled 311 Data Pull

**Purpose**: Validate whether the UC-01 ingestion requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and ready for implementation and review.
**Created**: 2026-03-11
**Feature**: [spec.md](/root/311-forecast-system/specs/001-pull-311-data/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [X] CHK001 Are requirements defined for how the configured schedule is established or referenced, rather than only stating that one exists? [Completeness, Gap, Spec §FR-001]
- [X] CHK002 Are the minimum required validation rules for dataset structure, required fields, data types, and completeness fully enumerated? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004]
- [X] CHK003 Are the conditions for creating a new dataset version versus leaving the current dataset unchanged explicitly defined for all run outcomes? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-008]
- [X] CHK004 Are the required contents of a failure notification record specified beyond run reference and failure category? [Completeness, Gap, Spec §FR-011]
- [X] CHK005 Are requirements defined for the observable run details needed to support acceptance assertions on timestamps, record counts, and status outcomes? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-010, Spec §SC-004]

## Requirement Clarity

- [X] CHK006 Is "latest 311 service request records since the last successful pull" defined unambiguously enough to avoid conflicting interpretations of the pull window? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-003]
- [X] CHK007 Is "configured schedule" precise enough to distinguish production scheduling requirements from test-trigger assumptions? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-001, Spec §Assumptions & Dependencies]
- [X] CHK008 Is "queryable" defined with sufficient precision to indicate which current-dataset facts must be retrievable for verification? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-012]
- [X] CHK009 Is "enough run detail" quantified or exemplified clearly enough to make logging requirements objectively reviewable? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-010]
- [X] CHK010 Is "partial or candidate dataset" defined consistently enough to distinguish rejected data, unstored data, and stored-but-inactive data? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-007, Spec §SC-003]

## Requirement Consistency

- [X] CHK011 Do the failure-handling requirements align between the user stories, edge cases, and functional requirements without leaving any failure path with a different activation rule? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-006]
- [X] CHK012 Are the success-path statements about storing, validating, and activating a dataset consistent between the spec and the planned data model? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005, Spec §Key Entities]
- [X] CHK013 Do the monitoring expectations remain consistent between the spec, success criteria, and plan artifacts, especially for failed runs? [Consistency, Spec §FR-011, Spec §SC-004, Gap]
- [X] CHK014 Are the terms "current dataset," "active dataset," and "current dataset marker" used consistently enough to avoid separate interpretations? [Consistency, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing, Spec §Key Entities]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [X] CHK015 Can each measurable outcome be objectively assessed from requirement text alone without relying on implied implementation details? [Acceptance Criteria, Measurability, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [X] CHK016 Is "in one run without manual intervention" measurable enough to determine what constitutes a completed successful run? [Clarity, Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001]
- [X] CHK017 Does the spec define how "100% of tested failure paths" maps to the governing acceptance scenarios so the requirement set is fully traceable? [Traceability, Spec §SC-002, Spec §SC-003]
- [X] CHK018 Are the acceptance scenarios complete enough to support every listed functional requirement, including run observability and notification recording? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-011]

## Scenario Coverage

- [X] CHK019 Are requirements specified for the primary success flow, no-new-records alternate flow, and all listed failure flows with no missing terminal state? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [X] CHK020 Does the spec explicitly address whether repeated failed runs before the next success should accumulate notifications, logs, or other retained run history? [Coverage, Gap]
- [X] CHK021 Are recovery expectations after a failed run sufficiently defined, or is recovery intentionally deferred beyond preserving the last known good dataset? [Recovery Flow, Gap, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-011]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [X] CHK022 Are requirements defined for what qualifies as "no new records" versus an empty but malformed or incomplete response? [Edge Case, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [X] CHK023 Are requirements defined for how unexpected schema changes are distinguished from other validation failures for reporting and monitoring purposes? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-010]
- [X] CHK024 Are boundary conditions addressed for the first-ever successful pull when no previous successful pull window exists? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-003]
- [X] CHK025 Are requirements defined for what happens if the previously active dataset does not exist when a run fails? [Edge Case, Gap]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [X] CHK026 Are performance expectations for scheduled pull completion and observability response time quantified enough to support review and prioritization? [Non-Functional, Clarity, Gap, Plan §Technical Context]
- [X] CHK027 Are reliability requirements defined for retry behavior, timeout thresholds, or explicit non-retry behavior where source availability failures occur? [Non-Functional, Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [X] CHK028 Are security requirements for credential handling, access to run-status data, and access to failure notifications sufficiently specified for this feature’s scope? [Non-Functional, Security, Gap, Plan §Constitution Check]
- [X] CHK029 Are auditability expectations for logs and monitoring records defined clearly enough to support operational review after failed runs? [Non-Functional, Observability, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-011]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [X] CHK030 Are all external dependencies that materially affect UC-01 behavior explicitly documented, including the Edmonton data source, scheduling service, storage system, and monitoring mechanism? [Dependency, Spec §Assumptions & Dependencies]
- [X] CHK031 Are the assumptions in [assumptions.md](/root/311-forecast-system/docs/architecture/assumptions.md) fully validated against the spec so they do not introduce hidden behavior requirements? [Assumption, Consistency]
- [X] CHK032 Does the spec make clear which requirements are mandatory for UC-01 now versus deferred to later forecasting or alerting features? [Scope Boundary, Spec §Summary, Gap]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [X] CHK033 Is there any unresolved ambiguity between "failure notification is recorded" in the use case contract and the spec’s looser wording around monitoring visibility? [Ambiguity, Conflict, Spec §FR-011]
- [X] CHK034 Are any requirements relying on vague adjectives such as "latest," "current," or "enough" without explicit decision rules or measurable criteria? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-012]
- [X] CHK035 Is a requirement identifier and traceability approach sufficient to link each acceptance test back to one or more specific requirements without gaps? [Traceability, Spec §Functional Requirements, Spec §Success Criteria]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Use this checklist to review requirement quality, not implementation behavior
